questions.question: Why don't neural nets consume raw waveforms well?
options: Floats can't be fed to neural nets

160,000 floats of a 10s clip are almost uncorrelated with the label — two identical phonemes 100ms apart have completely different raw samples; spectrograms collapse perceptually irrelevant jitter and keep the energetic-frequencies-over-time structure
Waveforms are too short for convolutions
Raw audio is always copyrighted
questions.explanation: The information is there but in the wrong form. The spectrogram aligns the representation with what perception (and the label) actually depends on.

questions.question: Why warp the frequency axis to the mel scale?
options: It makes the FFT faster
Humans perceive pitch logarithmically — 100 vs 200 Hz sounds the same distance apart as 1000 vs 2000 Hz; mel (m = 2595·log10(1 + f/700)) matches that, roughly linear below 1 kHz and logarithmic above
Mel removes background noise
It's required by the WAV format
questions.explanation: 80 mel bins covering 0-8 kHz is the standard ASR input. The mel-scaled spectrogram has been the single most important feature in speech ML from 2010 through 2026.

questions.question: How is a mel spectrogram computed from the STFT?
options: By resampling the waveform twice

A bank of triangular filters spaced equally on the mel scale, each a weighted sum of adjacent FFT bins — multiplying the STFT magnitude by the filterbank matrix gives the mel spectrogram in one matmul, then log(mel + 1e-10)
By transposing the spectrogram
Through a learned neural projection only
questions.explanation: Log-mel is the universal 2026 audio frontend — Whisper's input, Parakeet's input, SeamlessM4T's input. The log compresses 5-6 orders of magnitude of raw STFT dynamics.

questions.question: What are MFCCs, and where are they still used?
options: A lossless audio codec from the 90s
DCT (type II) of the log-mel spectrogram keeping the first 13 coefficients — decorrelates and compresses; dominant until ~2015, still used in speaker recognition (x-vectors, ECAPA)
The phase component of the STFT
A GPU kernel for spectrograms
questions.explanation: CNNs/Transformers on raw log-mels caught up after 2015, but the compact decorrelated form survives where small, robust speaker embeddings matter.

questions.question: What is the time-frequency resolution trade in STFT parameters?
options: There is no trade — bigger is always better

Larger FFT windows give better frequency resolution but worse time resolution: 25ms/10ms is the audio-ML default, 50ms/12.5ms suits music, 5ms/2ms suits transient detection like drum hits and plosives
Hop length only affects file size
Resolution is fixed by the sample rate alone
questions.explanation: Window choice matters too: the Hann window (multiplied per frame before the FFT) removes the spectral leakage caused by truncating at non-zero endpoints.
